整套首页
unit_01 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_01

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 501 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 SE
Though Edmund certainly had a dignified bearing and made a great first impression, those who became acquainted with him soon realized he had an essentially ______ nature.Though Edmund certainly had a dignified bearing and made a great first impression, those who became acquainted with him soon realized he had an essentially ______ nature.
答案：A, F　pugnacious; truculent
bearbearing
bear: standard unit headword | bearing: standard unit headword
#2 TC TRIPLE
Computers make it spectacularly easy to search for particular pieces of information in downloaded texts. And doing research in this strategic, targeted manner can feel (i)______. Instead of (ii)______ the organizing logic of the book you are reading, you can approach the book with your own questions and (iii)______. You, not the author, are the master.Computers make it spectacularly easy to search for particular pieces of information in downloaded texts. And doing research in this strategic, targeted manner can feel (i)______. Instead of (ii)______ the organizing logic of the book you are reading, you can approach the book with your own questions and (iii)______. You, not the author, are the master.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　empowering; surrendering to; glean precisely what you want from it
masterempowerdisregard
master: standard unit headword | empower: standard unit headword | disregard: standard unit headword
#3 TC DOUBLE
Folmer’s book on Edith Wharton seems far removed from recent trends in literary criticism; this need not to be a fault, except that, in its title and introduction, the book (i)______ to be conversant with contemporary discourse in the field, but in its actual analysis of Wharton’s work, it is marked by a very (ii)______ approach.Folmer’s book on Edith Wharton seems far removed from recent trends in literary criticism; this need not to be a fault, except that, in its title and introduction, the book (i)______ to be conversant with contemporary discourse in the field, but in its actual analysis of Wharton’s work, it is marked by a very (ii)______ approach.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　purports; old-fashioned
faultcontemporary
fault: standard unit headword | contemporary: standard unit headword
#4 TC SINGLE
A writing system that was not ____________ but deliberately formed on the basis of other scripts was introduced under the ancient Indian King Asoka, who ruled most of the Indian subcontinent from c. 268 to 232 B.C.E.A writing system that was not ____________ but deliberately formed on the basis of other scripts was introduced under the ancient Indian King Asoka, who ruled most of the Indian subcontinent from c. 268 to 232 B.C.E.
答案：D　indigenous
comprehensiveesoteric
comprehensive: standard unit headword | esoteric: standard unit headword
#5 SE
Today, the chair of the task force in charge of revising psychiatry diagnostic manual is a ______ post – people work for years to position themselves as candidates – but in the early nineteen, descriptive psychiatry was a backwater.Today, the chair of the task force in charge of revising psychiatry diagnostic manual is a ______ post – people work for years to position themselves as candidates – but in the early nineteen, descriptive psychiatry was a backwater.
答案：A, E　a coveted; an enviable
backwatercharge
backwater: standard unit headword | charge: standard unit headword
#6 TC DOUBLE
Trying to fix problems that affect vast numbers of people has an intuitive appeal that politicians and policy makers find (i)______, but several warehouses of research studies show that intuition is often a poor guide to fixing (ii)______ problems.Trying to fix problems that affect vast numbers of people has an intuitive appeal that politicians and policy makers find (i)______, but several warehouses of research studies show that intuition is often a poor guide to fixing (ii)______ problems.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　irresistible; systemic
intuitivesystemicirresistible
intuitive: standard unit headword | systemic: standard unit headword | irresistible: standard unit headword
#7 TC TRIPLE
The purpose of the Endangered Species Act, which was signed into law in 1973, is to conserve species at risk of extinction and the ecosystems upon which these species depend. (i) __________ a requirement simply to prevent extinction, the Act entails an (ii) __________ mandate: to "use ... all methods and procedures which are necessary to bring any [listed] species ... to the point at which the measures provided pursuant to this [Act] are no longer necessary." According to the Act, in other words, conservation requires the (iii) __________ of listed species.The purpose of the Endangered Species Act, which was signed into law in 1973, is to conserve species at risk of extinction and the ecosystems upon which these species depend. (i) __________ a requirement simply to prevent extinction, the Act entails an (ii) __________ mandate: to "use ... all methods and procedures which are necessary to bring any [listed] species ... to the point at which the measures provided pursuant to this [Act] are no longer necessary." According to the Act, in other words, conservation requires the (iii) __________ of listed species.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　More than; affirmative; recovery
entailaccord
entail: standard unit headword | accord: standard unit headword
#8 TC SINGLE
One of the most appealing things about Sloterdijk's philosophy is his willingness to be __________: he does not attempt to anticipate and to refute all possible objections.One of the most appealing things about Sloterdijk's philosophy is his willingness to be __________: he does not attempt to anticipate and to refute all possible objections.
答案：C　vulnerable
accessibleimpartial
accessible: standard unit headword | impartial: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　feeble; robust
feeblelopsided

答案：C, E　succumb to; yield to
biaspartiality
bias: standard unit headword | partiality: standard unit headword

答案：E　abbreviated
abbreviateremarkable
abbreviate: standard unit headword | remarkable: standard unit headword

答案：B, F　flummoxed; confounded
childbearingodds

Blank 2
答案：B, F　sound; intrinsically

答案：B　elide
disregardmirror

#17 SE
The Chavez Pass archaeological site was initially interpreted as indicative of ______ society, since it was thought to have been at the center of a cluster of smaller, contemporary settlements that it presumably controlled.The Chavez Pass archaeological site was initially interpreted as indicative of ______ society, since it was thought to have been at the center of a cluster of smaller, contemporary settlements that it presumably controlled.
答案：B, F　a hierarchical; a stratified
settlementcontemporary
settlement: standard unit headword | contemporary: standard unit headword

Engel’s task in writing a book about women in Russia must have been a (i)______ one, because the (ii)_______ the Russian empire’s people meant that Russian women could never be treated as a homogeneous group.Historian Barbara A. Engel’s task in writing a book about women in Russia must have been a (i)______ one, because the (ii)_______ the Russian empire’s people meant that Russian women could never be treated as a homogeneous group.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　daunting; diversity of
homogeneous

#19 SE
The uniquely human ability to rethink and revise our social arrangements is a weird blessing, allowing us to create systems that are as likely to _______ us as to liberate us.The uniquely human ability to rethink and revise our social arrangements is a weird blessing, allowing us to create systems that are as likely to _______ us as to liberate us.
答案：B, E　shackle; stifle
blessingshackle
blessing: standard unit headword | shackle: standard unit headword

答案：B　equable
gradient
gradient: standard unit headword

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　opulence; ascetic; respite from
deprivation

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　meeting; difficult; challenging
misguided

#24 TC SINGLE
Paintings created in India during the Mughal dynasty were ______ in ambition but ornamental in presentation: in one direction they have an affinity with newspaper photographs, while in the other they have the intricacy of jewels.Paintings created in India during the Mughal dynasty were ______ in ambition but ornamental in presentation: in one direction they have an affinity with newspaper photographs, while in the other they have the intricacy of jewels.
答案：B　documentary
ornamental

答案：D, F　realism; verisimilitude
instrumental

Blank 2
Blank 3

intimidate: standard unit headword
#53 SE
Although the author affects a breezy, ironic tone in much of his writing, his __________ gives way to palpable sadness when he writes about his experiences during the First World War.Although the author affects a breezy, ironic tone in much of his writing, his __________ gives way to palpable sadness when he writes about his experiences during the First World War.
答案：C, D　insouciance; detachment
palpable
palpable: standard unit headword
#54 TC DOUBLE
Some academic criticism of popular novels has been (i)______ in character, being based on the assumption that the wider the appeal, the more (ii)______ the novel.Some academic criticism of popular novels has been (i)______ in character, being based on the assumption that the wider the appeal, the more (ii)______ the novel.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　elitist; undesirable

comprehensible: standard unit headword

One view of historicism holds that systems of belief prevalent during different period in history are ______ and therefore cannot be understood except in their own term.One view of historicism holds that systems of belief prevalent during different period in history are ______ and therefore cannot be understood except in their own term.
答案：B　incommensurable
discriminatory
discriminatory: standard unit headword

The artists sought to (i) __________ precisely the impulses that traditional or generally accepted thinking had (ii) __________. By cultivating instinctive, irrational awarenesses--which might come from dreams or from accepting results derived by chance or from a receptivity to taboo--they hoped to (iii) __________ their audience's sense of everyday reality.The artists sought to (i) __________ precisely the impulses that traditional or generally accepted thinking had (ii) __________. By cultivating instinctive, irrational awarenesses--which might come from dreams or from accepting results derived by chance or from a receptivity to taboo--they hoped to (iii) __________ their audience's sense of everyday reality.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　affirm; marginalized; unsettle

The researcher noted that microbes, though _______, make up far more of the living protoplasm on earth than all humans, animals and plants combined.The researcher noted that microbes, though _______, make up far more of the living protoplasm on earth than all humans, animals and plants combined.
答案：A, B　diminutive; minuscular
omnipresent
omnipresent: standard unit headword
#58 TC SINGLE
In its literature and its political discourse, the nation has created various narratives about itself that tend to ______ intractable social divisions in the interest of perpetuating a dubious myth of unity.In its literature and its political discourse, the nation has created various narratives about itself that tend to ______ intractable social divisions in the interest of perpetuating a dubious myth of unity.
答案：B　obscure

explicate: standard unit headword

Memoirs are inherently (i)______, but Larry McMurtry’s volume of reminiscences about his life with books—not as a novelist but as a reader and bookstore owner—is especially (ii)______: nearly every page sounds a note of farewell to an age of books that he sees as passing.Memoirs are inherently (i)______, but Larry McMurtry’s volume of reminiscences about his life with books—not as a novelist but as a reader and bookstore owner—is especially (ii)______: nearly every page sounds a note of farewell to an age of books that he sees as passing.

Blank 2
答案：B, F　wistful; valedictory

Blank 3

答案：A　disparaged

#64 TC DOUBLE
The most (i) __________ weather phenomenon one might observe on Mars is a dust storm. No one knows when a dust storm might hit or how severe it will be, but the worst such storms can (ii) __________ the planet's entire surface in a cloud of yellow dust.The most (i) __________ weather phenomenon one might observe on Mars is a dust storm. No one knows when a dust storm might hit or how severe it will be, but the worst such storms can (ii) __________ the planet's entire surface in a cloud of yellow dust.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　dramatic; obscure

答案：E　paradigmatic

For many years, Americans have had a love affair with ferryboats. Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we’ve stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)______ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of our cars. Unfortunately, this (ii)______ notion recently has (iii)______ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.For many years, Americans have had a love affair with ferryboats. Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we’ve stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)______ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of our cars. Unfortunately, this (ii)______ notion recently has (iii)______ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　reduce; misguided; captivated
misguided
misguided: standard unit headword

Weeds lack the lethality of insects and microbes, which can strike farm crops seemingly out of nowhere and rapidly wipe them out. It is worth bearing in mind, however, that infestations of insects and disease are typically (i) __________ events, whereas weeds are (ii) __________.

Blank 2
答案：A, F　sporadic; ubiquitous

Photography (ii)______ modern arts, but when it is used as a tracing tool in order to (iii)______ the difficulties of achieving correct proportion, the resulting art often feels static and lifeless.

答案：B　esoteric

If we are to avoid a calamitous loss of biodiversity, according to Golbe, we cannot simply let nature take its course. Ongoing conservation management is (ii)______, as we have, in a sense, become nature, and the responsibilities that come with the role cannot be (iii)_______.Given our species’ increasing numbers and appetites which are reflected in and compounded by global climate change, even (i)______ species are likely to become endangered within the foreseeable future. If we are to avoid a calamitous loss of biodiversity, according to Golbe, we cannot simply let nature take its course. Ongoing conservation management is (ii)______, as we have, in a sense, become nature, and the responsibilities that come with the role cannot be (iii)_______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　now common; obligatory; jettisoned

accord: standard unit headword

Misunderstandings, blind alleys, and mistakes of various sorts will fail to appear in the final written accounts, because (ii)______ is a desirable attribute when transmitting results in a science report and would be poorly served by (iii)______.

They teach the reader that a struggle against severe difficulties in life is (i)______, that it is an intrinsic part of human existence, and that if one does not (ii)______, but steadfastly meets unexpected and often unjust hardships, one masters all obstacles and at the end (iii)______.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　unavoidable; shy away; emerges victorious

It is not just the entry of new collectors into the field that is causing this intensification. Established collectors’ interests are also becoming more (ii)______. Those who once concentrated on the work of either the nineteenth-century pioneers or the twentieth-century modernists are now keen to have (iii)______ collections.Now that photographic prints have become a popular field for collecting, auctions are becoming more (i)______. It is not just the entry of new collectors into the field that is causing this intensification. Established collectors’ interests are also becoming more (ii)______. Those who once concentrated on the work of either the nineteenth-century pioneers or the twentieth-century modernists are now keen to have (iii)______ collections.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　competitive; wide-ranging; comprehensive

Blank 3

Most populations are affected by a combination of adverse human pressures, each of which is in itself insufficient to (i)______ a population crash. Therefore, studies of population decline that (ii)______ individual factors and thus (iii)______ potential interactions may lead to improper management of declining species.Human-caused disturbances, such as habitat destruction and the introduction of nonnative species, are among the leading causes of plant and animal population declines. Most populations are affected by a combination of adverse human pressures, each of which is in itself insufficient to (i)______ a population crash. Therefore, studies of population decline that (ii)______ individual factors and thus (iii)______ potential interactions may lead to improper management of declining species.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　trigger; focus on; overlook

#103 TC DOUBLE
To the avid reader of E. O Wilson, much of his most recent book Consilience: The Unity of Knowledge will be (i)______, as the book represents the culmination of a life spent thinking about everything from the social lives of ants to the social lives of people. Nonetheless, new thoughts have been mixed in with the old to produce a book remarkable for its (ii)______ and ambition.To the avid reader of E. O Wilson, much of his most recent book Consilience: The Unity of Knowledge will be (i)______, as the book represents the culmination of a life spent thinking about everything from the social lives of ants to the social lives of people. Nonetheless, new thoughts have been mixed in with the old to produce a book remarkable for its (ii)______ and ambition.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　predictable; purview

remarkable: standard unit headword

Common and easily accessible resources (prey for predators or hosts for parasites) should be, all other things being equal, used frequently, yet in some environments apparently accessible and suitable resources remain ______.Common and easily accessible resources (prey for predators or hosts for parasites) should be, all other things being equal, used frequently, yet in some environments apparently accessible and suitable resources remain ______.
答案：E　unexploited
accessible
accessible: standard unit headword
#105 SE
Even though his opponent is currently trying to portray him as a wild-eyed radical, voters will likely reject this charge because it does not ______ his moderate political record.Even though his opponent is currently trying to portray him as a wild-eyed radical, voters will likely reject this charge because it does not ______ his moderate political record.
答案：B, F　conform to; square with
charge
charge: standard unit headword
#106 TC TRIPLE
Compared with, say, the precision of a skein of geese winging its way across the autumn sky in a V formation, the seasonal marches of grazers across the Serengeti seem (i)______, and at times even (ii)______. But, in fact, years of careful observation by scientists have shown that there is (iii)______ those migrations of zebras, wildebeests, and Thomson’s gazelles.Compared with, say, the precision of a skein of geese winging its way across the autumn sky in a V formation, the seasonal marches of grazers across the Serengeti seem (i)______, and at times even (ii)______. But, in fact, years of careful observation by scientists have shown that there is (iii)______ those migrations of zebras, wildebeests, and Thomson’s gazelles.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　disorganized; chaotic; a definite order to

disorganized: standard unit headword

Catherine the Great comes across in her memoirs as (i)______ ruler with a razor-sharp intellect, letting nothing stand in the way of her ambitions. In short, the impression the memoirs give is entirely in accord with her reputation for being (ii)______.Catherine the Great comes across in her memoirs as (i)______ ruler with a razor-sharp intellect, letting nothing stand in the way of her ambitions. In short, the impression the memoirs give is entirely in accord with her reputation for being (ii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　an uncompromising; formidable

accord: standard unit headword

Even though the authors repeatedly ______ their own shrewdness, they show a remarkable credulousness toward far-fetched ideas such as carbon-eating trees and cloud-making machinery.Even though the authors repeatedly ______ their own shrewdness, they show a remarkable credulousness toward far-fetched ideas such as carbon-eating trees and cloud-making machinery.
答案：B　extol
remarkable
remarkable: standard unit headword
#109 SE

答案：B, F　canned; formulaic
formulaic
formulaic: standard unit headword

If you wish to make a novel (i)______ a field of study, you must master what scholars have already said on the subject but at the same time keep in mind the (ii)______ received opinion, which can (iii)______ a nascent idea before it can develop.If you wish to make a novel (i)______ a field of study, you must master what scholars have already said on the subject but at the same time keep in mind the (ii)______ received opinion, which can (iii)______ a nascent idea before it can develop.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　contribution to; oppressive influence of; quash
master
master: standard unit headword
#111 TC DOUBLE

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　immersed in; radicalism

答案：B　accessible

#113 SE
Circulatory systems on organisms originated in widely separated epochs, according to the fossil record, and under a broad range of circumstances; the myriad forms they take attest to that ______ of origin.Circulatory systems on organisms originated in widely separated epochs, according to the fossil record, and under a broad range of circumstances; the myriad forms they take attest to that ______ of origin.
答案：D, F　heterogeneity; diversity

However, Kenny would have added significantly to his book’s value had he more effectively (ii)______ the influence of ancient philosophy on the subsequent tradition. As it is, newcomers to the subject will have little (iii)______ the afterlife enjoyed by ancient philosophy in the period 1600-1750.In this single volume, Kenny aims to survey for the general reader all of ancient philosophy. Understandably, space in such a book is (i)______ and he is not to be faulted for minor omissions. However, Kenny would have added significantly to his book’s value had he more effectively (ii)______ the influence of ancient philosophy on the subsequent tradition. As it is, newcomers to the subject will have little (iii)______ the afterlife enjoyed by ancient philosophy in the period 1600-1750.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　at a premium; signaled; sense of

答案：D　homogeneous

答案：A, F　bypass; circumvent

Media reports regularly imply that industry support of scientific work is alone sufficient to (ii)______ that research. 答案：B, D　unwarranted; groundless

However, other scholars emphasize the (i)______ of this view, empirically demonstrating that while economic development does in fact contribute to the well-being of the population of developing countries, the magnitude of development's positive effects on well-being has (ii)______. In other words, these scholars suggest that (iii)______ economic development and human well-being is taking place in developing countries.Firebaugh and Beck contend that economic development improves the overall well-being of people within developing countries. However, other scholars emphasize the (i)______ of this view, empirically demonstrating that while economic development does in fact contribute to the well-being of the population of developing countries, the magnitude of development's positive effects on well-being has (ii)______. In other words, these scholars suggest that (iii)______ economic development and human well-being is taking place in developing countries.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　limitation; decreased over time; a decoupling of

Blank 2

If the study proves that bears are still endemic to the area, the proposal to introduce additional bears of the same species will probably face less opposition, since the plan would then involve ____ a historic population, not trying to build population from scratch.If the study proves that bears are still endemic to the area, the proposal to introduce additional bears of the same species will probably face less opposition, since the plan would then involve ____ a historic population, not trying to build population from scratch.

答案：B, E　a hodgepodge; a patchwork

#130 TC TRIPLE
To get funding and tenured positions, medical researchers have to get their work published in well-regarded journals, where rejection can climb above 90 percent. Not surprisingly, the studies that tend to make the grade are those that make (i)______ claims. But while coming up with such (ii)______ claims is relatively easy, getting the data to bear them out is another matter. When studied rigorously, the great majority of these claims (iii)______.To get funding and tenured positions, medical researchers have to get their work published in well-regarded journals, where rejection can climb above 90 percent. Not surprisingly, the studies that tend to make the grade are those that make (i)______ claims. But while coming up with such (ii)______ claims is relatively easy, getting the data to bear them out is another matter. When studied rigorously, the great majority of these claims (iii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　eye-catching; striking; yield contradictory data

Nevertheless, it avoids the overly earned scolding tone common to many such endeavors.Because the book is largely concerned with an examination of various (i)______ often encountered in contemporary thinking, such as an exaggerated appreciation for meaningless coincidence and a credulous acceptance of pseudosciences, much of the writing has a (ii)______ quality to it. Blank 2

答案：E　partiality

Communal feeding is a remarkable behavioral aspect of this generally solitary animal. It is also misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Far from being a (i)______, communal devil feeding is (ii)______ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)______ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.Communal feeding is a remarkable behavioral aspect of this generally solitary animal. It is also misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Far from being a (i)______, communal devil feeding is (ii)______ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)______ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　free-for-all; structured; ritualized

I do not consider such worries (ii)______, though they are often based on intuitive feeling rather than on strictly logical arguments.

But as photography moves into the digital realm, family albums and home videos seem capable of (ii)______; our capacity to store them is, for all practical purposes, approaching the infinite. Is such a transformation a good thing? The natural world teaches us that (iii)______ are vital to ecological health. Does a similar principle apply to communal memory?Until now, old snapshots and home movies faded and crumbled and were eventually (i)______. Only a few precious mementos were preserved and passed along. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　discarded; immortality; death and decay

答案：B, E　evasive; elusive

On the one hand, high self-esteem is believed to confer a wide variety of adaptive and affective benefits; on the other, positive self-views have been linked to a number of intrapersonal and interpersonal strategies that can reasonably be described as (ii) __________.High self-esteem has recently assumed a somewhat (i) __________ status in social psychology. On the one hand, high self-esteem is believed to confer a wide variety of adaptive and affective benefits; on the other, positive self-views have been linked to a number of intrapersonal and interpersonal strategies that can reasonably be described as (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　paradoxical; antisocial
esoteric
esoteric: standard unit headword
#302 SE
The author's comparisons of Sontag's work to that of Walter Benjamin are __________: while the former is admittedly an influential figure, the latter is undoubtedly one of the most important critics of his era.The author's comparisons of Sontag's work to that of Walter Benjamin are __________: while the former is admittedly an influential figure, the latter is undoubtedly one of the most important critics of his era.
答案：A, B　overblown; inflated
overblown

And though audiences were sometimes unsettled when they found themselves thrust into the midst of the action, on balance they saw this as adding to the (iii) __________ of the performance.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　plastic; dictated by; excitement

Taruskin is punctilious, and justifiably proud, in emphasizing that his book is a work of history, not a survey of the repertory. Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　despite; disarms; ignored

Blank 2

Quite apart from a reasonable wariness of paint loss, sheer loyalty to the (ii) __________ look of a work, dirt and all, prejudices many. 答案：C, E　uniform; homogeneous

Philosophy is generally considered (i) __________ business in that it is pursued by thinkers constructing and evaluating arguments according to dispassionate considerations of soundness. In this view, the flesh-and-blood men and women involved--their personalities and histories--are (ii) __________ their philosophical claims. Yet some mavericks argue that philosophical positions are (iii) __________ the individuals who hold them and are enriched by biographical information.Philosophy is generally considered (i) __________ business in that it is pursued by thinkers constructing and evaluating arguments according to dispassionate considerations of soundness. In this view, the flesh-and-blood men and women involved--their personalities and histories--are (ii) __________ their philosophical claims. Yet some mavericks argue that philosophical positions are (iii) __________ the individuals who hold them and are enriched by biographical information.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　an impersonal; irrelevant to; inseparable from
accord
accord: standard unit headword

When bones (ii) __________, it triggers specialized cells to build bone. That's why astronauts, in the weightless environment of the space capsule, lose bone mass. When bones (ii) __________, it triggers specialized cells to build bone. #343 TC TRIPLE
Gravitational waves—ripples in the geometry of space-time—are analogous to electromagnetic waves. The challenge in trying to observe these waves directly is that they are extremely weak. To make waves large enough to be (i)______, the most (ii)______ events in the universe are required: supernova explosions, the formation of black holes, or the collision of stars. Even so, the effects are (iii) ______. The geometry changes so little that a distance of several kilometers changes by less than the diameter of a proton.Gravitational waves—ripples in the geometry of space-time—are analogous to electromagnetic waves. The challenge in trying to observe these waves directly is that they are extremely weak. To make waves large enough to be (i)______, the most (ii)______ events in the universe are required: supernova explosions, the formation of black holes, or the collision of stars. Even so, the effects are (iii) ______. The geometry changes so little that a distance of several kilometers changes by less than the diameter of a proton.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　detectable; violent; minuscule
explicable
explicable: standard unit headword

This seems (i)______ conclusion in hindsight, but its implication—that geological processes in the distant past must have (ii)______ these very same laws—was (iii)______ geologists in the eighteenth and nineteenth centuries.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　an obvious; followed; revolutionary for
entail
entail: standard unit headword

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　trust; a confident; exaggerate their certainty
disregard
disregard: standard unit headword
#395 SE
Upon taking office as president, he became __________ of a vigorous and energetic government, a stance that seemed at odds with earlier statements of his views, in which he had advocated strictly limited national government.Upon taking office as president, he became __________ of a vigorous and energetic government, a stance that seemed at odds with earlier statements of his views, in which he had advocated strictly limited national government.
答案：B, D　an exponent; a champion
odds
odds: standard unit headword
#396 TC TRIPLE
Although Sapir and Whorf’s notion that different languages may impart different cognitive skills met with (i) early on, they had little evidence to support their claim. By the 1970s many scientists had become (ii) their hypothesis, and it was largely (iii) as a new set of theories claiming that language and thought are universal came to dominate the field.Although Sapir and Whorf’s notion that different languages may impart different cognitive skills met with (i) early on, they had little evidence to support their claim. By the 1970s many scientists had become (ii) their hypothesis, and it was largely (iii) as a new set of theories claiming that language and thought are universal came to dominate the field.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　excitement; disenchanted with; abandoned
revive
revive: standard unit headword
#397 SE
Edited collections of scholarly essays generally tend to be somewhat uneven: they suffer from the _______ subject matter of the various essays, the lack of an overarching and consistent thesis, and the variable quantity of the contributions.Edited collections of scholarly essays generally tend to be somewhat uneven: they suffer from the _______ subject matter of the various essays, the lack of an overarching and consistent thesis, and the variable quantity of the contributions.
答案：B, C　disparate; heterogeneous
comprehensiveintrigue
comprehensive: standard unit headword | intrigue: standard unit headword
#398 TC TRIPLE
Although often seen as simply an inefficiency, redundancy in language actually serves as the (i) __________ to (ii) __________. It provides second chances, in other words. Every natural language has redundancy built in; this is why people can understand text (iii) __________ and why they can understand conversation in a noisy room.Although often seen as simply an inefficiency, redundancy in language actually serves as the (i) __________ to (ii) __________. It provides second chances, in other words. Every natural language has redundancy built in; this is why people can understand text (iii) __________ and why they can understand conversation in a noisy room.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　antidote; confusion; riddled with errors

precursor: standard unit headword
#399 SE
The ______ nature of the approval process for new drugs suggests that incentives to promote more expeditious decision making may be necessary.The ______ nature of the approval process for new drugs suggests that incentives to promote more expeditious decision making may be necessary.
答案：D, F　sluggish; dilatory
arbitrary
arbitrary: standard unit headword
#400 TC TRIPLE
Writing about Jamaican poet Una Marson in 1937, scholar Philip Sherlock (i) ___________ the nationalistic feeling with which Marson's poems are imbued. Although this quality of Marson's poetry struck Sherlock as a sign of her emotional generosity (he remarked on her great "love of her homeland and its people"), Sherlock's (ii) ___________ was not (iii) ___________: he found some of Marson's poems excessive in their emotionality.Writing about Jamaican poet Una Marson in 1937, scholar Philip Sherlock (i) ___________ the nationalistic feeling with which Marson's poems are imbued. Although this quality of Marson's poetry struck Sherlock as a sign of her emotional generosity (he remarked on her great "love of her homeland and its people"), Sherlock's (ii) ___________ was not (iii) ___________: he found some of Marson's poems excessive in their emotionality.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　noted; approbation; unqualified
inexplicable
inexplicable: standard unit headword
#401 SE
Compared to their predecessors, who were more nationalist than feminist in political orientation, Roman women activists of the younger generation are more inclined to ______ women’s rights, often creating organizations focused primarily on women’s issues.Compared to their predecessors, who were more nationalist than feminist in political orientation, Roman women activists of the younger generation are more inclined to ______ women’s rights, often creating organizations focused primarily on women’s issues.
答案：C, F　champion; defend
de-emphasize
de-emphasize: standard unit headword
#402 TC TRIPLE
In an age obsessed with rankings, we should resist the tendency to rate a composer by the number of hits he or she achieves. Music that is (i) ____________ may well be (ii) ____________, no matter how we define the term, even if this quality does not (iii) ____________ popularity or commercial success.In an age obsessed with rankings, we should resist the tendency to rate a composer by the number of hits he or she achieves. Music that is (i) ____________ may well be (ii) ____________, no matter how we define the term, even if this quality does not (iii) ____________ popularity or commercial success.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　unfashionable; brilliant; translate into

accessible: standard unit headword
#403 SE

答案：C, E　minimal; trifling

intrigue: standard unit headword
#404 TC TRIPLE
Since her death in 1960, Zora Neale Hurston's literary reputation has (i) ____________; Hurston died in obscurity, but she now has the status of a major American author. However, despite her (ii) ____________ oeuvre, the (iii) ____________ her reputation is based mainly on Their Eyes Were Watching God, her second novel, which has stimulated more scholarly writing than all of Hurston's other books combined.Since her death in 1960, Zora Neale Hurston's literary reputation has (i) ____________; Hurston died in obscurity, but she now has the status of a major American author. However, despite her (ii) ____________ oeuvre, the (iii) ____________ her reputation is based mainly on Their Eyes Were Watching God, her second novel, which has stimulated more scholarly writing than all of Hurston's other books combined.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　undergone a dramatic change; substantial; growth of
esoteric
esoteric: standard unit headword
#405 SE
In Inuit culture, elaborate carving has often been used to enhance ______ objects such as harpoon heads and other tools.In Inuit culture, elaborate carving has often been used to enhance ______ objects such as harpoon heads and other tools.
答案：A, B　utilitarian; functional
ornamental
ornamental: standard unit headword
#406 TC TRIPLE
While physicists consider the Navier-Stokes equations used to model the movement of fluids to be (i) __________, mathematicians are (ii) __________ them. To a mathematician, it means little that the equations (iii) __________. They want proof that the equations are unfailing: that no matter the fluid, and no matter how far into the future you forecast its flow, the mathematics of the equations will still hold.While physicists consider the Navier-Stokes equations used to model the movement of fluids to be (i) __________, mathematicians are (ii) __________ them. To a mathematician, it means little that the equations (iii) __________. They want proof that the equations are unfailing: that no matter the fluid, and no matter how far into the future you forecast its flow, the mathematics of the equations will still hold.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　reliable; skeptical regarding; appear to work

intrigue: standard unit headword
#407 SE
There are great ______ in countries’ greenhouse gas emissions, especially in per capita terms; while the United States and China are similar in aggregate emissions, United States’ per capita emissions are a huge multiple of China’s.There are great ______ in countries’ greenhouse gas emissions, especially in per capita terms; while the United States and China are similar in aggregate emissions, United States’ per capita emissions are a huge multiple of China’s.
答案：B, E　disparities; variances
vacillation
vacillation: standard unit headword
#408 TC TRIPLE
Among chess aficionados, there have been many doomsday scenarios about people (i) __________ the game as a result of the superiority of computer chess programs over human players. Others have replied to this with variation on the theme of how we still hold footraces despite cars going much faster, but this analogy is (ii) __________ since cars do not help humans run faster while chess computers undoubtedly have an effect on the (iii) __________ of human chess.Among chess aficionados, there have been many doomsday scenarios about people (i) __________ the game as a result of the superiority of computer chess programs over human players. Others have replied to this with variation on the theme of how we still hold footraces despite cars going much faster, but this analogy is (ii) __________ since cars do not help humans run faster while chess computers undoubtedly have an effect on the (iii) __________ of human chess.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　losing interest in; spurious; quality
master
master: standard unit headword
#409 SE
There is frequently a protracted time interval between the introduction of an innovative musical composition and its public acceptance: the concert-going public often spurns the ______ in favor of the familiar for a prolonged period.There is frequently a protracted time interval between the introduction of an innovative musical composition and its public acceptance: the concert-going public often spurns the ______ in favor of the familiar for a prolonged period.
答案：C, D　novel; original

comprehensive: standard unit headword
#410 TC TRIPLE
That the ancient Greeks did not understand tides is (i) __________: for the Greeks, and the Romans after them, the tidal oscillations of the Mediterranean were so (ii) __________ that they were frequently (iii) __________ the effects of local winds and weather.That the ancient Greeks did not understand tides is (i) __________: for the Greeks, and the Romans after them, the tidal oscillations of the Mediterranean were so (ii) __________ that they were frequently (iii) __________ the effects of local winds and weather.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　unsurprising; minimal; obscured by

inexplicable: standard unit headword
#411 SE
In science, replicability comes with the idea that the pursuit of scientific truth should not be ______: researchers who make claims must allow others to test them empirically.In science, replicability comes with the idea that the pursuit of scientific truth should not be ______: researchers who make claims must allow others to test them empirically.
答案：C, E　private; insular
settled
settled: standard unit headword
#412 TC TRIPLE
The poem is not nearly as (i) __________ as it has often been said to be. Granted, it would be (ii) __________ to claim that the poem is self-contained or that its proliferating allusions are self-explanatory. But the poem is more self-contained than it appears when you read only fragments of it, and its allusions are often (iii) __________ by their contexts or by their repeated appearances in changing contexts.The poem is not nearly as (i) __________ as it has often been said to be. Granted, it would be (ii) __________ to claim that the poem is self-contained or that its proliferating allusions are self-explanatory. But the poem is more self-contained than it appears when you read only fragments of it, and its allusions are often (iii) __________ by their contexts or by their repeated appearances in changing contexts.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　difficult; absurd; explained
accessible
accessible: standard unit headword
#413 SE
One of the peculiarities of humans is that we irrationally gravitate to the predictable and avoid risk; whatever the reasons for this ______, it is hardly a sound basis for dealing with complex, long-term problems.One of the peculiarities of humans is that we irrationally gravitate to the predictable and avoid risk; whatever the reasons for this ______, it is hardly a sound basis for dealing with complex, long-term problems.
答案：B, D　predilection; proclivity
vacillation
vacillation: standard unit headword
#414 TC TRIPLE
Researchers have tried, with little success, to gain an understanding of how brain activity in roundworms gives rise to various rudimentary behaviors. The (i) __________ establishing a link between biology and behavior in humans is (ii) __________. While the media routinely report on scans showing that specific brain locations light up when we feel particular emotions or perform particular actions, the impression given by these news stories--that current technology provides real insights into how the brain works--is (iii) __________.Researchers have tried, with little success, to gain an understanding of how brain activity in roundworms gives rise to various rudimentary behaviors. The (i) __________ establishing a link between biology and behavior in humans is (ii) __________. While the media routinely report on scans showing that specific brain locations light up when we feel particular emotions or perform particular actions, the impression given by these news stories--that current technology provides real insights into how the brain works--is (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　difficulty of; still more acute; specious
remarkable
remarkable: standard unit headword
#415 SE
Joshua Gisemba Bagaka found that the pedagogical results of group projects and other engaged learning activities in Kenyan mathematics classrooms were ______; such activities, then, may not be the best way of improving mathematics education.Joshua Gisemba Bagaka found that the pedagogical results of group projects and other engaged learning activities in Kenyan mathematics classrooms were ______; such activities, then, may not be the best way of improving mathematics education.
答案：C, E　mixed; inconsistent

inexplicable: standard unit headword
#416 TC TRIPLE
Unlike news reports, theater isn't expected to stick to the facts. Built on a sandy foundation of make-believe and (i) __________, the form, by nature, traffics in (ii) __________. Good documentary drama exploits its inherent paradox: creating artifice from verbatim texts, it uncovers truths by playing on the tension between what's real and what's (iii) __________.Unlike news reports, theater isn't expected to stick to the facts. Built on a sandy foundation of make-believe and (i) __________, the form, by nature, traffics in (ii) __________. Good documentary drama exploits its inherent paradox: creating artifice from verbatim texts, it uncovers truths by playing on the tension between what's real and what's (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　pretense; illusion; invented
remarkable
remarkable: standard unit headword
#417 SE
The author engages this issue from diverse perspectives, supports his arguments with many examples, and manages to avoid antagonizing others in dealing with a very ______ subject.The author engages this issue from diverse perspectives, supports his arguments with many examples, and manages to avoid antagonizing others in dealing with a very ______ subject.
答案：A, C　contentious; controversial
intrigue
intrigue: standard unit headword

A recent survey of 120 United States corporations found that a third of employees in blue-chip corporations wrote poorly in their electronic mail. Millions of these (i) __________ e-mail messages are clogging computers by setting off requests for (ii) __________, and many of the requests, in turn, are also (iii) __________ written, resulting in whole cycles of confusion.A recent survey of 120 United States corporations found that a third of employees in blue-chip corporations wrote poorly in their electronic mail. Millions of these (i) __________ e-mail messages are clogging computers by setting off requests for (ii) __________, and many of the requests, in turn, are also (iii) __________ written, resulting in whole cycles of confusion.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　inscrutable; elucidation; shoddily

The current _______ of repackaged music under Miles Davis’ name might prompt any reasonable person to conclude that the recording vault has been plundered bare.The current _______ of repackaged music under Miles Davis’ name might prompt any reasonable person to conclude that the recording vault has been plundered bare.
答案：A, D　glut; surfeit
revival
revival: standard unit headword
#420 TC TRIPLE
Unlike those glossy Architectural Digest photographs that are deliberately (i) __________ lest they distract from a focus on the building itself, Friedlander's architectural photography is the product of his insistence that he is an individual looking at buildings and scenes as they actually exist. Friedlander reminds us that everyday objects (ii) __________ or, more exactly, that the unimpeded visual scene of a building without cars or power lines or street signs is a highly (iii) __________ photographic artifact.Unlike those glossy Architectural Digest photographs that are deliberately (i) __________ lest they distract from a focus on the building itself, Friedlander's architectural photography is the product of his insistence that he is an individual looking at buildings and scenes as they actually exist. Friedlander reminds us that everyday objects (ii) __________ or, more exactly, that the unimpeded visual scene of a building without cars or power lines or street signs is a highly (iii) __________ photographic artifact.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　impersonal; normally interfere; contrived

intrigue: standard unit headword

If aging is merely an avoidable by-product of life rather than a necessary progression, it is possible that we might eventually forestall ______.If aging is merely an avoidable by-product of life rather than a necessary progression, it is possible that we might eventually forestall ______.
答案：A, C　senescence; decrepitude

privation: standard unit headword
#422 TC TRIPLE
Computers triumphed at chess not by (i) __________ human thought, but by playing like machines. The analogy with flight is (ii) __________: as long as people tried to fly by imitating birds, they were doomed to failure; once they (iii) __________ the paradigm of the familiar, however, they were soon flying much faster than birds.Computers triumphed at chess not by (i) __________ human thought, but by playing like machines. The analogy with flight is (ii) __________: as long as people tried to fly by imitating birds, they were doomed to failure; once they (iii) __________ the paradigm of the familiar, however, they were soon flying much faster than birds.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　aping; instructive; escaped

#423 SE
The book aims to illuminate how science has changed the meaning of nothingness from ______ philosophical concept to something we can almost put under a microscope.The book aims to illuminate how science has changed the meaning of nothingness from ______ philosophical concept to something we can almost put under a microscope.
答案：C, F　a nebulous; a vague
palpable
palpable: standard unit headword
#424 TC TRIPLE
Seigel has written an important and invaluable book on the history of Western ideas of the self, showing a genuine (i) __________ in analyzing philosophical views of often daunting intricacy. Though a historian by profession, he keeps to a minimum his excursions into the (ii) __________ of ideas, recognizing that great minds often (iii) __________ the preconceptions of their age.Seigel has written an important and invaluable book on the history of Western ideas of the self, showing a genuine (i) __________ in analyzing philosophical views of often daunting intricacy. Though a historian by profession, he keeps to a minimum his excursions into the (ii) __________ of ideas, recognizing that great minds often (iii) __________ the preconceptions of their age.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　acuity; social and cultural context; outstrip

timidity: standard unit headword
#425 SE
Although most scientists hold that quantum theory and the theory of general relativity ought to be intimately connected, the theories have remained stubbornly ______.Although most scientists hold that quantum theory and the theory of general relativity ought to be intimately connected, the theories have remained stubbornly ______.
答案：B, D　unlinked; estranged
arbitrary
arbitrary: standard unit headword
#426 TC TRIPLE
The author's new novel is everything that her first was not: obvious and (i) __________ rather than understated and subtly shaded. Instead of quietly revealing themselves through tiny, unexpected gestures, the characters are forced to make (ii) __________ statements that announce exactly what they are thinking and serve primarily to underscore the author's sociological points. There are occasional glimpses of the (iii) __________ and emotional wisdom so lavishly demonstrated in the author's previous novel--gifts, the reader hopes, that will be more fruitfully employed in her next.The author's new novel is everything that her first was not: obvious and (i) __________ rather than understated and subtly shaded. Instead of quietly revealing themselves through tiny, unexpected gestures, the characters are forced to make (ii) __________ statements that announce exactly what they are thinking and serve primarily to underscore the author's sociological points. There are occasional glimpses of the (iii) __________ and emotional wisdom so lavishly demonstrated in the author's previous novel--gifts, the reader hopes, that will be more fruitfully employed in her next.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　melodramatic; tendentious; psychological acuity
intuitive
intuitive: standard unit headword
#427 SE
Although one can adduce myriad examples of ecosystem disruption by non-indigenous species, nevertheless most introduced species that survive in fact appear to have quite ______ effects on the ecosystems they have invaded.Although one can adduce myriad examples of ecosystem disruption by non-indigenous species, nevertheless most introduced species that survive in fact appear to have quite ______ effects on the ecosystems they have invaded.
答案：A, B　minimal; trifling

#428 TC TRIPLE
When reviewing a book that advertises lying as its subject, the critic has a duty to screen the title for possible (i) __________, and it is therefore necessary to report that The Concise Book of Lying is not concise. If the book were making a case for deception, the mismatch might not matter: a (ii) __________ title might suit a book designed to disabuse us of our respect for (iii) __________. But the book is not an apology for lying: it is on the side of truth.When reviewing a book that advertises lying as its subject, the critic has a duty to screen the title for possible (i) __________, and it is therefore necessary to report that The Concise Book of Lying is not concise. If the book were making a case for deception, the mismatch might not matter: a (ii) __________ title might suit a book designed to disabuse us of our respect for (iii) __________. But the book is not an apology for lying: it is on the side of truth.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　ambiguity; misleading; veracity
partiality
partiality: standard unit headword
#429 SE
Because movie studios, under pressure to generate international sales, have favored big-budget pictures with fantasy plots, the representation of everyday domestic life has largely been ______ other media, such as television and literature.Because movie studios, under pressure to generate international sales, have favored big-budget pictures with fantasy plots, the representation of everyday domestic life has largely been ______ other media, such as television and literature.
答案：A, B　left to; ceded to

#430 TC TRIPLE
Cultures can shape attitudes and beliefs in ways that (i)______ conscious awareness or control; in other words, cultural orientations may develop from processes that do not entail (ii)______ participation, and cultures may pervade subtle psychological dynamics in ways that individuals may not be able to (iii)______. Thus, theories and tools developed to study implicit cognition may increase our understanding of the complex interplay between culture and individuals.Cultures can shape attitudes and beliefs in ways that (i)______ conscious awareness or control; in other words, cultural orientations may develop from processes that do not entail (ii)______ participation, and cultures may pervade subtle psychological dynamics in ways that individuals may not be able to (iii)______. Thus, theories and tools developed to study implicit cognition may increase our understanding of the complex interplay between culture and individuals.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　operate outside of; active; report

We do not always use words in accordance with their dictionary definitions, for meaning often fluctuates with context; that does not mean, however, that we are completely ______ in how we use language.We do not always use words in accordance with their dictionary definitions, for meaning often fluctuates with context; that does not mean, however, that we are completely ______ in how we use language.
答案：B, E　unconstrained; free

#432 TC TRIPLE
The motives of many major investors in Pop Art have arguably been, too a large extent, (i)______. These collectors demonstrate and enhance their power over the art market by establishing seemingly arbitrary works of art as priceless. This phenomenon reveals that (ii)______ is not (iii)______ of truth or beauty, but simply a trick of investment capital.The motives of many major investors in Pop Art have arguably been, too a large extent, (i)______. These collectors demonstrate and enhance their power over the art market by establishing seemingly arbitrary works of art as priceless. This phenomenon reveals that (ii)______ is not (iii)______ of truth or beauty, but simply a trick of investment capital.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　self-aggrandizing; value; a product

The sociologist argued that criminal behavior is an impermanent condition because it is the result of cyclical forces operating through ______ factors, not the manifestation of deeply rooted personal characteristics.The sociologist argued that criminal behavior is an impermanent condition because it is the result of cyclical forces operating through ______ factors, not the manifestation of deeply rooted personal characteristics.
答案：A, C　contingent; circumstantial

alarming: standard unit headword
#434 TC TRIPLE
Recent proposals for fixing the climate have taken the form of large-scale geoengineering projects such as launching mirrors into space to reflect solar radiation away from Earth, undertakings that are vastly more (i)______ than anything a nineteenth-century rainmaker could have cooked up. What is unclear, as one looks back at the history of weather modification research, is whether this resourceful ambition will be (ii)______, or if, by contrast, it serves to make the scientific community’s (iii)______ that much more devastating.Recent proposals for fixing the climate have taken the form of large-scale geoengineering projects such as launching mirrors into space to reflect solar radiation away from Earth, undertakings that are vastly more (i)______ than anything a nineteenth-century rainmaker could have cooked up. What is unclear, as one looks back at the history of weather modification research, is whether this resourceful ambition will be (ii)______, or if, by contrast, it serves to make the scientific community’s (iii)______ that much more devastating.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　enterprising; productive; impotence

#500 SE
For many adults, the adolescent years occupy (i) place in the memory, which to some degree is even quantifiable: give a grown adult a series of random prompts and cues, odds are he or she will recall (ii) number of memories from adolescence.For many adults, the adolescent years occupy (i) place in the memory, which to some degree is even quantifiable: give a grown adult a series of random prompts and cues, odds are he or she will recall (ii) number of memories from adolescence.
答案：B, D　a privileged; a disproportionate
oddsarbitrary
odds: standard unit headword | arbitrary: standard unit headword
#501 SE
Although the philosopher frequently dealt with __________ theoretical topics, she was capable of writing in a style that was comprehensible to a general audience.Although the philosopher frequently dealt with __________ theoretical topics, she was capable of writing in a style that was comprehensible to a general audience.
答案：B, E　abstruse; recondite
comprehensibleintrigue
comprehensible: standard unit headword | intrigue: standard unit headword
